target.name: Anduril Industries
target.founder: Palmer Luckey, Brian Schimpf, Trae Stephens, Matt Grimm, Joe Chen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $30.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Anduril Industries, a Series G defense and security company founded by Palmer Luckey, Brian Schimpf, Trae Stephens, Matt Grimm, and Joe Chen, has raised $2.5B+ to date and carries a current valuation of $78B+. The Defense & Security sector comprises 20 peers, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Scale AI, Saronic, and Shield AI, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier could not be constructed for median benchmarking.

Of the two valuation methods assessed, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method applies a standard 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement to the company's last post-money valuation of $30.5B, yielding an implied value of $38.1B. Peer-Median analysis was not executable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Defense & Security cohort; expanding to all-sector peers would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity.

The triangulated range supported by this analysis spans $32.4B (low) to $43.8B (high), with a median of $38.1B, derived from a single methodological input. This analysis is materially constrained by limited public and private peer coverage at Anduril's stage and scale. The current $78B+ valuation substantially exceeds the derived range, suggesting either undisclosed operational performance, market expectations for near-term inflection, or valuation multiples not reconcilable by standard stage-progression mechanics. Validation would require additional revenue run-rate data or transaction comparables in autonomous defense systems.
